hooks.comment: Marketplace-dev hook. The plugin's hooks/hooks.json registers the same hook for consumers via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}; both paths are safe because the script is idempotent.

Marketplace-dev hook (dev-mirror of the plugin's hooks/hooks.json entry). Advisory: warns when an edit to a plugin agent file looks like the agent is instructing itself to spawn a sub-agent. Set RC_GUARD_RECURSIVE_SPAWN_STRICT=1 to make it blocking.

Marketplace-dev hook (dev-mirror of the plugin's hooks/hooks.json entry). Advisory (Claim Grounding & Source Honesty): nudges on an unhedged absolute capability claim written into a knowledge/ or docs/ markdown file without a provenance marker. Opt-in, skips frontmatter/code/blockquotes/headings/conditionals, honors `claim-lint-ok`. Exit 0 always.

Marketplace-dev hook (dev-mirror of the plugin's hooks/hooks.json entry). Advisory (Capability Grounding — consult-your-access-inventory): nudges on a 'tell the user to manually check / open the portal' phrasing written into a knowledge/ or docs/ markdown file. Opt-in, skips frontmatter/code/blockquotes/headings, suppresses a hand-back reason or a line citing the held route, honors `delegation-nudge-ok`. Exit 0 always; cannot see the chat answer (written-artifact subset only).

Marketplace-dev hook (dev-mirror of the plugin's hooks/hooks.json entry). When a plugin.json or marketplace.json version changes inside this marketplace clone, regenerates the Copilot package + dashboard.html + repo-guide.html — the artifacts CI freshness gates check. No-op on non-manifest edits and when version is unchanged.

Marketplace-dev mirror of the plugin's PostToolUse WebFetch entry. Records the per-session 'seen' marker for a whitelisted domain ONLY after the fetch actually proceeded (PostToolUse doesn't fire on a blocked call) — the consent-ordering complement to guard-web-access.sh so a denied first fetch re-prompts instead of silently auto-allowing. Fail-safe; never blocks.
Block obviously destructive commands that slipped past the deny list. Reads the tool call as JSON on stdin; exits 2 to block (exit 1 would not block).

Marketplace-dev hook. Command review (the Thing) — T3 panel tribunal. No-ops (fast grep) unless a category has `thing: on` in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml. The internal panel deadline (~75s) stays under this 90s hook timeout. The plugin's hooks/hooks.json registers the same script for consumers via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}; both paths are safe because the script is idempotent and short-circuits when no toggle is set.

Marketplace-dev hook (dev-mirror of the plugin's hooks/hooks.json entry). Runaway/rabbit-hole brake — portable replacement for native auto-mode's 3/20 brake. No-ops unless .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml exists (this repo has none, so it no-ops); exits 2 when the agent thrashes (default 8 identical calls in a row) or blows a total-call ceiling (default 200). Deterministic, no model.
Layout enforcement: deny writes that don't match .repo-layout.json. Same script the plugin distributes to consumers.

Marketplace-dev mirror of the plugin's WebFetch entry. Website allow/deny enforcement: whitelisted domain auto-allows, blacklisted blocks (exit 2), unlisted falls through to the prompt. Reads .ravenclaude/web-access.yaml; no-ops if absent.

Marketplace-dev hook. Decision-review enforcement (the Thing, decision mode). No-ops (fast grep) unless `decision_review: advisory|binding` is set in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml (default off; this repo has no such file so it no-ops). When opted in AND the AskUserQuestion is a single binary non-multiselect yes/no-shaped question, routes it through thing-decide.py and on a BINDING yes/no DENIES the prompt with the verdict so the agent proceeds without interrupting the user; defer/advisory/high-blast/error -> allow. Same script the plugin distributes to consumers via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}.

Marketplace-dev hook (dev-mirror). Definition-of-done gate: no-ops unless definition_of_done.cmd is set in .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml (this repo has none, so it no-ops). When set + source changed, runs the gate command at Stop and blocks until it passes (self-limited to max_blocks). Closes the correctness/hallucination gap.
Remind the Team Lead to run the full test suite when source files changed. Advisory fallback when no definition_of_done.cmd is configured.

Marketplace-dev hook (dev-mirror). Agentic Work-Streams (P1): on session end, if a work-stream is active in .ravenclaude/streams/, append a derived session_closed event + refresh state.md (crash-resilience). DERIVED-ONLY, fail-safe, never blocks.

Marketplace-ONLY notification channel (deliberately NOT in any plugin hooks.json, so it never ships to consumers). Fires on the Claude Code Notification event and delivers the message to a sink: it ALWAYS appends a durable JSON line under .ravenclaude/runs/notifications/ (gitignored), and if RAVENCLAUDE_NOTIFY_WEBHOOK is set it also POSTs {"text":"<message>"} to that URL (Slack/Mattermost/Google-Chat-style incoming webhook). Fail-safe, always exits 0, never logs the webhook secret. This is the on-the-web / scheduled-routine egress that stands in for the managed PushNotification tool when it is absent in the environment.

Marketplace-dev hook (dev-mirror). Agentic Work-Streams P4 (OPT-IN, FAIL-OPEN): no-ops unless .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml sets stream_hook: per_prompt (this repo doesn't, so it no-ops). When opted in, attributes the prompt to the active stream as a derived event (sticky — no reclassify) or auto-sets on a confident match. NEVER blocks/alters the prompt; NO-EGRESS (only derived labels persist).

Marketplace-dev hook (dev-mirror). Agent-dispatch evaluator, Phase 3 — AUDIT-ONLY. No-ops (single grep) unless .ravenclaude/dispatch-config.json has enabled:true (this repo has none, so it no-ops). When opted in, shadow-logs a model-right-sizing verdict for the about-to-start subagent; it NEVER denies or redispatches (RM1 — SubagentStart fires post-spawn, deny-is-pre-commit unverified). The plugin's hooks/hooks.json registers the same script for consumers via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}; both paths are safe because the hook is fail-open and inert when dispatch-config is absent or disabled.

Marketplace-dev hook. Regenerates the comfort-posture project-layer rules from .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml at session start. No-ops in this repo because it has no .ravenclaude/comfort-posture.yaml. The plugin's hooks/hooks.json registers the same script for consumers via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}; both paths are safe because the script is idempotent and no-ops when the posture file is absent.

Marketplace-dev hook. Warns when a session loads in acceptEdits/bypassPermissions mode, which bypasses the comfort-posture rules. The plugin's hooks/hooks.json registers the same hook for consumers via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}; both paths are safe because the script is advisory and idempotent.

Marketplace-dev hook. Injects a session-start capability banner (project surface + detected auth by env-var NAME only + effective settings.json permissions + work-streams) via hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext. The only subprocess is a bounded read-only git read used by the streams classifier (gated on streams existing); the 10s timeout bounds any pathological read. The plugin's hooks/hooks.json registers the same script for consumers via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}; both paths are safe because it is read-only, fail-silent, and emits nothing when there is nothing to report.
